questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the figure that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
questions.solution:
From figure 1 to figure 2 is obtained by all the 3 elements exchange their position by rotating anticlockwise.

From figure 2 to figure 3 same pattern is follow the three elements rotate anticlockwise.

In the option D, the same pattern is followed i.e all elements rotate anticlockwise.

Hence, option D is correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q: In the given question, a part of the figure is missing. Select one of the answer figures which exactly fits into the missing part of the question figure.
questions.solution: There is a cross symmetry in the given question figure. The right image is exactly the mirror image of the left image. After observing all the given answer figures, we find that the answer figure 3 completes the question figure. Hence, this is the answer.

options: A

questions.q: Select the option that will fill in the blank and complete the given series.
questions.solution:
The central arrow is rotated $90^{\circ}$ in clockwise direction and the arrow in the top left corner and the star in the bottom right corner are interchanging the positions. Similarly, The shape in the bottom left corner and the triangle in the top right corner are interchanging their places.
Hence, the correct answer is "Option (3)".

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Consider the following figures:
Change in positions of beads in the four figures above follows a sequence. Following the same sequence, which of the figures below should appear as the fifth figure above?
questions.solution:
The given figure:
In the second figure, the position of the two balls in the left is interchanged and the position of the two balls in the right have been interchanged with respect to their positions in the first figure. In the third figure, the position of the two balls on the top is interchanged and the position of the two balls in the bottom is interchanged with respect to their positions in the second figure. Again, in the fourth figure, the position of the two balls in the left is interchanged and the position of the two balls in the right have been interchanged with respect to their positions in the third figure. Thus, in the fifth figure, the position of the two balls on the top is interchanged and the position of the two balls in the bottom is interchanged with respect to their positions in the fourth figure.
Hence, the second figure is the answer figure.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Find three figures out of the following five figures $1,2,3,4$ and 5 which when fitted into each other would form a complete square.
questions.solution: We begin with choosing a figure having a right angle. Out of the five figures (5) seems to be having two equal sides including a right angle. Fitting along (4) with it, we have a figure as now. Looking into a vacant space, we find that out of the remaining three figures (1), (2) and (3) only fig. (1) will fit, hence the answer is (2).
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
